name: n8n: Dockerized self-host stack
description: Self-hosted n8n automation layout with Docker Compose, backup scripts, reverse-proxy oriented folders, and maintenance assets—suitable for private workflow hosting.
longDescription: Self-hosted workflow automation using Docker Compose: service definitions, container context, scripts, and supporting static assets to run n8n on private infrastructure. Backup helpers and deployment-oriented directories are structured so secrets and internal endpoints stay in private configuration. The layout emphasises repeatable operations—start/stop, backups, upgrades—rather than embedding business-specific workflow logic in versioned files.
features: Compose-first layout for bringing automation online in a controlled environment
Backup script scaffolding for database snapshots
Supporting folders for proxy TLS material and maintenance pages (populated per operator)
Separation between runtime volumes and versioned configuration
highlights: Keeps workflow automation under your own Docker runtime
Sensitive hostnames and secrets stay in private configuration, not in public-facing descriptions
challenges: Self-hosted automation must stay patched, backed up, and reachable only to trusted networks.
solutions: Rely on compose with documented volumes, scripted backups, and TLS termination at the edge—never embedding secrets in public JSON.
security: Store webhook URLs, API keys, and DB passwords only in private env files
Restrict administrative ports via firewall or VPN
Rotate automation credentials on a schedule
